Bridelia or Bridelia tomentosa is a plant species that belongs to the family Phyllanthaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Bridelia tomentosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Bridelia tomentosa include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.

The classification of Brizopyrum capense reflects its evolutionary relationship to other plants within the Poaceae family.
